title: Unblocker: Perspective
description: Generates an original craft aphorism to reframe how you see the work.
entries: A scene without friction is a hallway; get your characters out of the hallway.
Plot is what happens; story is what it costs.
If everyone in the room agrees, you have written a memo, not a scene.
The reader forgives a slow start only if you never make them wait again.
Character is what someone does when the easy choice is also the wrong one.
Dialogue is two people trying to win; if nobody is trying to win, cut it.
You do not describe a room, you describe who is looking at it.
Tension is a promise; suspense is making the reader wait for you to keep it.
Every scene should end by opening a door you did not walk through yet.
Backstory is salt, not soup.
A detail earns its place by doing two jobs at once.
The first draft is you telling yourself the story; the second is you telling the reader.
Show the wound, not the diagnosis.
If you can cut a line and lose nothing, you never needed the line.
Conflict is not two people shouting; it is two people who both make sense.
The most interesting sentence in a paragraph is often the second one; start there.
A character who cannot be surprised cannot be alive.
Give the reader two things to want and make them mutually exclusive.
Mood is built from nouns and verbs; adjectives just take the credit.
The scene is not about the argument; it is about what the argument is protecting.
You earn a big feeling by underplaying the moment before it.
When you are stuck, you are usually protecting a character; stop protecting them.
Setting is a character that never leaves the room.
A good line of dialogue answers a question the reader did not know to ask.
Write toward the thing you are afraid to say, then say a little more.
Coincidence to cause trouble is fair; coincidence to solve it is theft.
The reader believes specific lies and doubts vague truths.
End the chapter one beat early and let the reader lean forward.
What a character refuses to say is louder than anything they do say.
Revision is not fixing sentences; it is finding out what the story was always about.
